data.title: DentScribe Launches AI Treatment Coordinator Notes for Dental Practices
data.description: A cutting-edge workflow has been developed to transform post-appointment discussions with patients into organized financial records, actionable items, and valuable business intelligence. DentScribe, based in Sunnyvale, California, revealed this innovation on April 1, 2026. The AI-powered program, designed for dental record-keeping and efficiency, will revolutionize the way dental professionals handle patient interactions. This new system is set to significantly enhance productivity and streamline administrative tasks in the dental field.

data.title: Nike, RH And 3 Stocks To Watch Heading Into Wednesday
data.description: U.S. stock futures are up today, with investors eyeing specific stocks. UniFirst Corp. (NYSE: UNF) is expected to announce earnings of $1.21 per share and revenue of $614.92 million before the market opens. UniFirst's stock closed at $251.59 on Tuesday, rising by 0.9%. On the other hand, RH (NYSE: RH) reported disappointing fourth-quarter results and predicted lower-than-expected sales for fiscal year 2026. The company foresees revenue between $3.58 billion and $3.72 billion for FY26. For more details, visit a financial news platform.

Nikkei reports that Asahi Kasei is entering the low-CTE (low thermal expansion) glass cloth market. Nitto Boseki currently holds ~90% global market share in this product. Asahi Kasei does not manufacture glass fiber itself — it sources fiber externally and specializes in weaving it into glass cloth.

data.title: CHC Navigation Announces Major Updates to i93, i85, i76 and iBase GNSS Receivers
data.description: CHCNAV has recently released updates for their i93, i85, and i76 GNSS receivers along with iBase. These updates are aimed at enhancing the stability of positioning, streamlining the setup process, and broadening the functionalities of the devices. Users can now benefit from improved accuracy and efficiency with these enhanced features.

data.title: Adaptability Revealed as the New Foundation of Job Security in the AI Age, According to 2026 ETS Human Progress Report
data.description: Today, ETS, a worldwide education and talent solutions organization, unveiled the 2026 ETS Human Progress Report in Princeton, N.J. This report marks the third year of the global study examining how individuals worldwide are managing swift workforce disruption and technological advancements.

data.title: Bisedge Appointed Exclusive Dealer for Linde Material Handling in South Africa
data.description: On April 1, 2026, in Johannesburg, Bisedge, a key player in eco-friendly logistics services, revealed its appointment as the sole distributor for Linde Material Handling goods in South Africa starting from the same date. This collaboration marks a significant milestone for both companies, solidifying their presence in the region and enhancing their offerings.

data.title: KAIJU NO. 8 THE GAME: Young Talents Awaken in Main Story Chapter 10. New ★5 Haruichi Izumo Debuts Alongside 1,500 Dimensional Crystal Rewards in New Chapter Event!
data.description: On April 1, 2026, in Los Angeles, KAIJU NO. 8 THE GAME, a collaboration between Akatsuki Games, TOHO, and Production I.G, unveiled the launch of Main Story Chapter 10 titled "Aptitude Test." This new chapter in the game brings focus to the emerging talents within the Japan Defense Force, as they navigate through challenges and obstacles.

data.title: TOKYO SKYTREE to Host First Collaboration with "BLUE LOCK" from April 9 to July 6, 2026
data.description: In Tokyo on April 1, 2026, TOBU TOWER SKYTREE Co., Ltd. announced their collaboration with the popular TV anime "BLUE LOCK" by Muneyuki Kaneshiro. The event, named "BLUE LOCK EPISODE SKY in TOKYO SKYTREE (R)," marks the first partnership between the tower and a TV anime. The unique event will take place at TOKYO SKYTREE, offering fans and visitors a chance to experience the world of "BLUE LOCK" in a new setting. Feel free to visit and enjoy this exciting partnership between the two entities.

data.title: Samsung Research Reveals Over Half of Europeans Admit to Looking at a Stranger's Phone Screen in Public – With Nearly a Quarter Doing so out of Sheer Curiosity
data.description: In a recent study by Samsung, it was discovered that in Europe, public spaces have turned into shared screens with 56% of individuals admitting to inadvertently glancing at a stranger's phone. Public transport stood out as the most common location for observing someone else's screen, with 57% of participants mentioning it. Surprisingly, 24% of Europeans confessed to deliberately peeking at another person's phone due to curiosity, leading to exposure of personal content ranging from private photos to sensitive bank information. As millions across the continent gear up for the Easter weekend, these insights shed light on the prevalence of phone snooping in everyday environments.

data.title: Airwallex Secures Key Regulatory Approvals for Full Commercial Launch in Malaysia
data.description: Airwallex, a prominent global financial and payments platform, has recently been granted approval by Bank Negara Malaysia for its e-money issuing and Class A licenses. This development allows Airwallex to offer a comprehensive range of payment services in Malaysia and enhances the capabilities for companies looking to grow internationally. The approval enables businesses to operate more smoothly in Malaysia, facilitating seamless cross-border transactions. This signifies a significant step towards facilitating economic growth and international business operations in Malaysia.
